steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 predicts that warming benefits invasive species over natives, based on non-boreal research. Text 2 tests this in Alaska and finds warming had no effect on either species. We need the choice that correctly characterizes how Text 2 relates to Text 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Text 2 describes a methodology that helps researchers avoid a problem encountered in the studies discussed in Text 1.: characterizes Text 2 as critiquing Text 1's methodology. Text 2 says nothing about how Text 1's research was conducted. ✗
  • Choice B — Text 2 explains a study that suggests an alternative explanation for the trend observed in the research discussed in Text 1.: frames Text 2 as offering an alternative explanation for invasion. Text 2 reports a null result, not an alternative cause. ✗
  • Choice C — Text 2 discusses an observation that challenges the validity of the findings described in Text 1.: says the non-boreal research is invalid. Text 2 only shows the findings may not apply to boreal contexts—not that the original research is wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — Text 2 presents a finding that casts doubt on the generalizability of the research discussed in Text 1.: says the Alaskan findings cast doubt on whether non-boreal results generalize to boreal contexts—exactly what a null result in a different ecosystem shows. ✓
That is the correct answer.
